description: The provided image displays a publisher "Check for updates" functional icon featuring a red bookmark symbol within a multicolored circular ring, rather than experimental scientific data. Based on the accompanying text extraction summary, the paper investigates multi-stage dynamic recrystallization (DRX) in low-alloy steel (0.398C-0.87Mn-0.31Si) under varying deformation conditions using an inhomogeneous-dislocation-density 3D cellular automaton model. The actual scientific figures in this work reportedly include flow stress-strain curves at 850-1150 degrees Celsius and strain rates of 0.01-10 s^-1, evolution of average grain size under transient conditions, and EBSD-based prior austenite grain reconstructions with grain orientation spread (GOS) analysis. The figure likely compares experimental hot compression results with CA simulations for DRX fraction, grain size distribution, and flow stress evolution under both constant and transient temperature/strain rate paths. The material system involves hot compression at 850-1050 degrees Celsius with varying strain rates (0.001-1 s^-1) and temperature transitions. However, these scientific details are not directly visible in the provided icon image and are derived solely from the textual context.
